description: Native app surfaces gating (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): the `android` and `ios` mobile-web gates are validated on the general matrix. This spec is deterministic on EVERY host — it asserts the always-SKIP phase gates (android+browser -> A5, ios+browser -> A5), which return before any SDK/emulator/simulator detection, so no host lazily installs a toolchain or boots a device here. Native mobile app runs (which PASS on capable hosts: Android under A3b, iOS under A4) are exercised in the apps-android and apps-ios groups, not here.

tests.description: An android context with a browser step is SKIPPED pointing at phase A5 (mobile web on Android). This gate fires before any SDK detection, so the outcome is deterministic on every host and never triggers a toolchain install or emulator boot.

An ios context with a browser step is SKIPPED pointing at phase A5 (mobile web on iOS). This gate fires before any simulator detection, so the outcome is deterministic on every host and never boots a simulator. Native iOS app runs (PASS on capable macOS hosts under A4) are exercised in the apps-ios group.
